content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Closed the remaining 9ni.5.7 create-time hardening gaps for same-bead project reuse. `src/cli/project.rs` now fails closed when a bead-backed project is already in `running` state but milestone lineage has no matching active row, returning remediation guidance instead of allowing a shadow project. The same-bead project lookup was also widened from a single preferred project to a scan across all matching bead-backed projects, with ordering that prefers non-terminal states over completed history; this keeps `project create-from-bead` from ignoring failed/not-started retries just because the default project already completed, while still allowing creation when every prior same-bead project is terminal. `find_existing_bead_project` now uses that shared scan as well, so milestone adoption inherits the same non-terminal preference instead of relying on the old single-project heuristic. Deterministic regressions were added for the running-without-lineage repair path, the completed-default-plus-failed-retry case, the all-terminal-retries allowance, and the internal selector preference.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Reviewed the pending amendments against the current duplicate same-bead protections and confirmed two remaining gaps: `RunStatus::Running` without active lineage still allowed shadow-project creation, and same-bead project discovery still relied on a single preferred project instead of evaluating all matching attempts.
2. [Completed] Updated `src/cli/project.rs` to treat a same-bead running project without matching lineage as repair-required inconsistent state, and refactored same-bead project discovery into a full matching-project scan with status-aware ordering that prefers non-terminal retries over completed history while allowing creation only when all matches are terminal.
3. [Completed] Kept milestone adoption aligned by routing `find_existing_bead_project` through the same multi-project scan and priority rules, so consumers such as `src/cli/milestone.rs` now adopt the most relevant non-terminal same-bead project instead of depending on the old default-project shortcut.
4. [Completed] Added deterministic regression coverage for the running-without-lineage repair case, the completed-default plus failed-retry selection case, the all-terminal same-bead allowance, and the selector preference helper, then ran the required repository verification gates.

## Validation Evidence

- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub project_create_from_bead_reports_running_same_bead_project_without_active_lineage` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub project_create_from_bead_prefers_failed_retry_over_completed_default_project` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub project_create_from_bead_allows_creation_with_multiple_terminal_same_bead_projects` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub find_existing_bead_project_prefers_non_terminal_retry_over_completed_default` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check` exited successfully.
- `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings` exited successfully.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` passed on the full tree.
- `nix build` passed on the final tree.

## Outstanding Risks

None identified.
